entities.name: LABORATORY INFORMATION MANAGEMENT SOFTWARE
entities.description: A software system designed to track samples and manage data throughout the genotyping process to ensure quality and efficiency.

entities.name: GENOTYPING WORKFLOWS
entities.description: The structured sequence of steps and protocols used to perform genotyping.

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT CROP GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of genotyping large numbers of crop samples using automated technologies.

entities.name: DNA SEQUENCER-BASED TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies utilizing DNA sequencers to automate steps in the genotyping process and increase throughput.

entities.name: GENOTYPING PROCESS
entities.description: The overall procedure of determining the genetic constitution of an organism.

entities.name: GENOTYPIC DATA
entities.description: The large amounts of genetic information generated during the genotyping process.

entities.name: QUALITY CONTROL
entities.description: The process of ensuring the accuracy, reliability, and standardization of genotypic data.

entities.name: GOOD LABORATORY PRACTICE
entities.description: A set of standards for laboratory operations, including protocol standardization and data annotation.

entities.name: CROP
entities.description: Agricultural plants that are the subject of high throughput genotyping.
